title: pfaffia grandiflora var. typica
intro: Pfaffia grandiflora var. typica, also known as the suma plant, is a type of plant native to the Amazon rainforest in South America, whose name comes from the Pfaff family, who first discovered the plant. The suma plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Pfaffia grandiflora var. typica include reducing inflammation and pain, improving immune function, and increasing energy levels, making it a popular herbal remedy for conditions such as arthritis, fatigue, and stress. Herbal preparations of the suma plant include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
